a:r.a:t: Sales Executives | External Compensation Plan Assessment
HGV has a more complex commission structure
(5-8 tiers) compared to market competitors (3-5 tiers), reducing simplicity and line-of-sight for reps.
Market plans rely more heavily on SPIFFs, team bonuses, and non-monetary rewards
to drive short-term behaviors and retain top performers.
Compared with HGV’s standard payout approach,
some market plans also use true-ups, guarantees
, or other earnings-stabilization features to reduce income volatility and support retention
Total Compensation Range (i.e., Leverage / Actual potential earnings)
In exceptional cases top-end upside can reach $500K in stronger sales environments
5-8 commission tiers with each tier reflecting increased volume achievement and incremental commission %
Additional commission tiers add 1-10% to base commission
3-5 commission tiers with each tier reflecting increased volume achievement and incremental commission %
Annual true-ups to stabilize earnings, and
sometimes guaranteed earnings to smooth monthly incentive variability
SPIFFs: Drive immediate behavior changes (same-day/over few days)
Budgets are small and typically a fixed dollar amount
STIs: Run for longer durations (up to ~3 months) to encourage behavior not built into the plan
Directors/VPs control SPIF/STI budgets but approval is required prior to launch
SPIFFs (cash bonuses per deal, gift cards, rewards points)
Non-monetary: Contests, prizes, time-off perks, and experiential rewards
used to boost engagement and retention
*Represents actual earnings data from 1/1/2025 through 12/31/2025, however does not represent mid-year job changes, and therefore might not be accurate for every employee
Where HGV Varies from the Market
